articles.title: 3 WAYS ASANA MULTIPLIES MARKETING RESULTS WITH AI
articles.summary: As a marketer, your job _should_ be doing the creative work that moves the needle. But the reality is, too much of your time is taken up by chasing approvals, searching for updates, and figuring out blockers.

articles.title: THE BRAND TAX: HOW GOOGLE PROFITS FROM DEMAND YOU ALREADY OWN
articles.summary: Paid search performance looks strong because branded search takes credit for demand that already exists. An analysis of 99B sessions finds that ad costs rose 30%, cost-per-visit rose 9.4%, and conversion rates fell 5.1%, while paid search bounce rates hit 59% and paid social reached 65%. Branded campaigns claim 1,299% ROAS versus 68% for non-branded, yet branded keywords rarely drive new revenue. Most conversions come from repeat visitors, while paid channels bring low quality traffic that leaves quickly. As search shifts beyond Google and AI answers reduce clicks, investing in brand visibility and AI SEO can lower wasted spend and improve overall returns.

articles.title: PINTEREST SPRING TREND REPORT 2026: PERSONALIZATION IS IN BLOOM
articles.summary: Pinterest trends show a shift from perfection to feel-good living. People are embracing self-expression at home with bold colors and small upgrades. Food trends favor comfort and creativity over dieting. Social moments are more casual with low-effort gatherings. Activities are more local with small outdoor spaces and simple escapes. Cleaning and organizing are now framed as self-care. Overall, the focus is on improving daily life, not reinventing it.

articles.title: YOUTUBE SOCIAL LISTENING: HOW TO FIND ACTIONABLE INSIGHTS IN A VIDEO-FIRST WORLD
articles.summary: YouTube social listening requires a different approach than text-based platforms because API restrictions limit comment data collection at scale. Instead of chasing volume, marketers can focus on comments across their own videos, competitor channels, and creator content to surface product feedback, content requests, and sentiment shifts. A structured strategy involves categorizing comments into themes like pain points, purchase-intent signals, and competitor comparisons, then translating those patterns into content and positioning decisions.

articles.title: WHY GMAIL IS CLIPPING YOUR EMAILS — AND WHAT TO DO ABOUT IT
articles.summary: Gmail automatically hides any part of an email that exceeds 102 KB, showing a "Message clipped" link that most readers never click. This quietly damages open rates because the tracking pixel typically sits at the bottom of the email and gets cut along with the hidden content. Common culprits include uncompressed images, heavy inline CSS, and long newsletters with multiple formatted sections.

articles.title: STOP RANKING. START GETTING RECOMMENDED.
articles.summary: LLMs don't crawl rankings. They pull from mentions, credible signals, and buyer language. Sydney Sloan (CMO Advisor, G2) breaks down how to earn visibility where B2B decisions actually get made.

articles.title: _SIGN UP FOR THE LIVE PLAYBOOK SESSION (WEBINAR) _→
HOW TO BUILD A PROFITABLE PERSONAL BRAND (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This webinar with Matt Gray on March 24 will teach founders how to build a profitable personal brand using systems instead of constant effort. It covers content strategy, growth frameworks, and simple team structures that drive revenue.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC ACADEMY
articles.summary: Anthropic Academy offers 13 free courses from beginner to developer level. The courses cover AI fluency, Claude Code, and more.

articles.title: ‘AI-DESIGNED' HURTS SALES
articles.summary: Research shows that labeling products as “AI-designed” can hurt sales, with purchase intent dropping by up to 29% compared to human-designed items and people perceiving 75% less human involvement. This happens because consumers struggle to connect with products that lack a human creator, especially when identity plays a role in the purchase. However, framing AI as a tool used by humans improves outcomes, with human-AI collaboration increasing purchase intent by 3.5% over fully human designs and 12.8% over AI-only ones.

articles.title: WHY CMOS AND ICS SEE AI SO DIFFERENTLY
articles.summary: While 83% of marketers say leadership is committed to AI, 62% of CMOs report being able to prove ROI compared to just 12% of ICs. Leaders focus on efficiency and outcomes, while ICs deal with workflow challenges, governance delays, and untracked effort. This divide is driven by gaps in visibility and ownership, which leads leaders to push scale while teams grow cautious.

articles.title: AMAZON EXPECTED TO MOVE PRIME DAY SALE TO JUNE
articles.summary: Amazon is expected to move Prime Day from July to late June, shifting the sales surge into the second quarter and changing revenue timing. The timing is critical for third-party sellers and competing brands. With Amazon accounting for about 40% of US ecommerce spend, the timing shift could impact the broader retail calendar, especially around back-to-school and holiday planning.

articles.title: TECHNICAL SEO TIP: THE NEW YORK TIMES' BRILLIANT SITEMAP
articles.summary: The New York Times "Today" sitemap allows search engines to immediately crawl and index their highest priority content.

articles.title: OSCARS RATINGS DOWN 9 PERCENT FROM LAST YEAR, SMALLEST AUDIENCE SINCE 2022
articles.summary: The decline largely mirrors drops at the Golden Globes and Grammys earlier this year, suggesting a broader linear TV trend.
